key_insight: A microstructure-conditioned hybrid surrogate, trained via a hypernetwork, can predict multiscale behavior of mycelium composites across varying microstructures with far less data than standard surrogates.
engineering_relevance: This enables tractable multiscale optimization of graded sustainable materials and can meaningfully reduce stress concentrations in designed structures.
method: Condition a hybrid physics-data microscale surrogate on microstructural variables using a hypernetwork.
Use the surrogate inside multiscale optimization to evaluate functionally graded designs efficiently.
Extend conditioning to manufacturing variables to link process settings to macroscopic response.
evidence_signal: The approach was validated against full FE^2 simulation and used to optimize a graded disk, reducing peak stress by 42% versus a random microstructure.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the training set covers the space of microstructures and manufacturing conditions, so extrapolation beyond seen designs is a risk.
paper_title: Microstructure-Conditioned Surrogate Models for Graded Multiscale Optimization of Mycelium Composites
